# Paper 29 χ² recomputation on the i.i.d. branch

## Inputs

- IO i.i.d. branch exact evaluation values:
  - `H0 = 67.57585653582628`
  - `Omega_m = 0.34868395067621694`
  - `Omega_k = -0.04579112576013168`
  - `Omega_r = 0.0001226`
  - `Omega_lambda_display = 0.6971071750839146`
  - `Omega_lambda_eval = 0.6969845750839148`
  - `r_d = 143.3 Mpc`

- Planck LCDM fixed reference:
  - `H0 = 67.4`
  - `Omega_m = 0.315`
  - `Omega_k = 0.0`
  - `Omega_r = 9.19480093248451e-05`
  - `Omega_lambda_display = 0.685`
  - `Omega_lambda_eval = 0.6849080519906752`
  - `r_d = 147.09 Mpc`

## Dataset summary

- CC35: `35` points from `Jia et al. (2025), MNRAS 542, 1063, Table A1`
- DESI DR2 GCcomb: `13` points with full covariance from `DESI DR2 GCcomb Gaussian likelihood (official mean/covariance files)`
- Combined: `48` points

## χ² summary

| dataset | N_data | dof | IO χ² | IO χ²/dof | Planck χ² | Planck χ²/dof | Δχ² = IO - Planck |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| CC | `35` | `35` | `14.701661702497464` | `0.42004747721421326` | `15.079021457867908` | `0.43082918451051166` | `-0.37735975537044375` |
| BAO | `13` | `13` | `94.36149688498531` | `7.258576683460409` | `28.693892246596665` | `2.207222480507436` | `65.66760463838865` |
| Combined | `48` | `48` | `109.06315858748277` | `2.2721491372392246` | `43.77291370446457` | `0.9119357021763452` | `65.2902448830182` |

## Replacement of dead Schur numbers

| dataset | old dead Schur χ² | new i.i.d. χ² | delta new-old |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| CC | `15.57` | `14.701661702497464` | `-0.868338297502536` |
| BAO | `26.91` | `94.36149688498531` | `67.45149688498532` |
| Combined | `42.48` | `109.06315858748277` | `66.58315858748279` |

## Residual tables

- full CC residuals: `paper29_iid_branch_cc_residuals.csv`
- full BAO residuals: `paper29_iid_branch_bao_residuals.csv`

## Notes

- BAO total χ² uses the full published covariance.
- The BAO row table records raw residuals and diagonal-error naive pulls for inspectability; those are not the total correlated χ² contributions.
- The evaluator includes the requested radiation term. Because the user-specified branch display values omit radiation from closure, `Omega_lambda_eval` is closure-corrected so that `E(0)=1`.
